title: PressProbe Properties
description:
PressProbe Properties

PressProbe Properties

The following list is a summary of properties for the PressProbe object.  The details for each property are explained in the Description field.

Property	Description

Name Property	This property can set a particular name that is assigned to objects.  When PressProbe object is created, name is assigned automatically.  For the assigned name, number is added after PressProbe such as PressProbe01.

You can change the name and up to 32 characters can be specified.  English one byte characters and underscore [ _ ] are available.  Also, you cannot use number for initial character.

Description Property	This property can set explanations for objects.  The explanation can be freely written using up to 255 characters.

Enabled Property	This property can set whether to enable the object.  When specifying True, the program executes the object.  When specifying False, the program executes the next object without executing the object.  Use this property when you want to save the sequence temporary without executing it or try with different parameters by copying objects during sequence creation.

AutoStepID Property	This property can set an automatic assignment of StepID in object execution.  When specifying True, assign StepID automatically.  When specifying False, it will be the value specified by StepID Property.  When assigning automatically, (sequence number *100 + object number) is set.  StepID is recorded to files that the force or the position during the sequence execution is recorded.  It is used to determine which interval corresponds to which object.

StepID Property	This property can set StepID during the object execution.  It is only used when AutoStepID is False.

AbortSeqOnFail Property	This property can set operations when the object fails.  When specifying True, the program ends the sequence and proceeds to the next SPEL statement if the object fails.  When specifying False, the program proceeds to the next force guide object without ending the sequence even if the object fails.  It is used when you want to continue the sequence (ex: the recovery processes are included in the sequence when the object fails.)

IOPreprocessEnabled Property	This property can set I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying True, the program executes I/O operation.  When specifying False, the program does not execute I/O operation.  I/O operations are defined by IOPreprocOutputBit Property and IOPreprocOutputStatus Property.  It is used when you want to operate the hands or peripherals before the object execution.

IOPreprocOutputBit Property	This property can set I/O operations (output bit) when the object starts.  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

IOPreprocOutputStatus Property	This property can set output status in I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying On, set the specified output bit to On (set to 1).  When specifying Off, set the specified output bit to Off (set to 0).  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

ProbeTrajectory Property	This property can set trajectory of the object.  When specifying Straight, move on the straight line trajectory and pause at a hole or an obstacle.  When specifying Spiral, move on the spiral trajectory and pause at a hole or an obstacle.

ProbeDetectType Property	This property can set the target type that PressProbe object detects.  When specifying Hole, detect a hole position on the trajectory to be searched.  When specifying Obstacle, detect an obstacle position on the trajectory to be searched.

AccelS Property	This property can set the acceleration when moving on the specified trajectory.

SpeedS Property	This property can set the speed when moving on the specified trajectory.

SpiralDiam Property	This property can set the diameter of the spiral trajectory.  It is used when specifying Spiral in ProbeTrajectory property.

SpiralPitch Property	This property can set the pitch of the spiral trajectory.  It is used when specifying Spiral in ProbeTrajectory property.  When decreasing SpiralPitch, moving around the same trajectory in the same range increases.  Therefore, the possibility that it cannot be detected without moving through the object decreases, however, the time to search increases.  When increasing SpiralPitch, moving around the same trajectory in the same range decreases.  Therefore, the possibility that it cannot be detected without moving through the object increases, however, the time to search decreases.

DestRelativeX Property	For the straight line trajectory, this property can set the moving amount in X direction of coordinate systems specified by ForceOrient and RobotLocal of the sequence.  It is used when specifying Straight in ProbeTrajectory property.

DestRelativeY Property	For the straight line trajectory, this property can set the moving amount in Y direction of coordinate systems specified by ForceOrient and RobotLocal of the sequence.  It is used when specifying Straight in ProbeTrajectory property.

DestRelativeZ Property	For the straight line trajectory, this property can set the moving amount in Z direction of coordinate systems specified by ForceOrient and RobotLocal of the sequence.  It is used when specifying Straight in ProbeTrajectory property.

PressForce Property	This property can specify the force to press.  The robot presses and searches to be the force specified in the PressForce property in PressOrient direction during the execution of PressProbe object.  When pressing to positive direction, value will be negative.  When pressing to negative direction, value will be positive.

PressOrient Property	This property can specify the direction to press.  You can set the direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  You can select from translation direction (+Fx ~ -Fz). The robot moves to the specified trajectory while pressing to the specified direction.  In the case of the spiral trajectory, generate the spiral trajectory on a plane perpendicular to the specified direction.

PressStiffnessGain Property

CFEnabled Property	This property set whether to continue the force control functions after the objects are ended.  When specifying True, the force control functions will continue even if the objects are ended.  When specifying False, the force control functions will end when the objects are ended.  When the sequence ends even if CFEnabled is True, the force control functions will end.

ForceCheckEnabled　Property	This property can set the end conditions of the object related to force.  When specifying True, the end conditions related to force are enabled.  When specifying False, the end conditions related to force are disabled.  The end conditions related to force are the conditions specified in ProbeDetectThresh.

ProbeDetectThresh Property	For the end conditions related to force, this property can set the threshold to be determined as detection.  When specifying Obstacle in ProbeTrajectory, specify positive value.  At this time, the end conditions of force are that the square-root of sum of squares of force other than the direction specified by PressOrient (ex: when selecting -Fz, sqrt(Fx*Fx+Fy*Fy)) exceeds the value specified by ProbeDetectThresh.  When specifying Hole in ProbeTrajectory and Fx, Fy, or Fz in PressOrient, specify the negative value.  At this time, the end conditions of force are that the force of the direction specified by PressOrient will be under the value specified by ProbeDetectThresh.  When specifying Hole in ProbeTrajectory and -Fx, -Fy, or -Fz in PressOrient, specify the negative value.  At this time, the end conditions of force are that the force of the direction specified by PressOrient will exceed the value specified by ProbeDetectThresh.

PosCheckEnabled Property	This property can set the end conditions of the object related to positions. When specifying True, the end conditions related to positions are enabled.  When specifying False, the end conditions related to positions are disabled.

PlaneNumber Property	This property can set the Plane number to be used of the end conditions related to positions.  When specifying Plane in the PlaneType property, Plane of the specified number will not be changed.  When specifying Relative in the PlaneType property, redefine a new Plane to the specified number each time the object is executed.  Therefore, note that the original setting will be lost.

PlaneEndCond Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the conditions to be determined as an end.  When specifying Inside, it is a condition that the robot enters into the specified Plane.  When specifying Outside, it is a condition that the robot is out of the specified Plane.

PlaneRelativeOrg Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can specify which coordinate system sets the relative position setting Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.  When specifying Base, you can set the relative position on Base coordinate system.  When specifying Local, you can specify the relative position on Local coordinate system.  The Local number is specified by PlaneRelativeRobotLocal.  When specifying Tool, you can set the relative position on Tool coordinate system.

PlaneRelativeX Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the relative position in X direction to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.

X direction follows the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrg.

PlaneRelativeY Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the relative position in Y direction to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.

Y direction follows the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrg.

PlaneRelativeZ Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the relative position in Z direction to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.

Z direction follows the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrg.

PlaneRelativeOrient Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the coordinate system to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.  Plane is set on axis specified by PlaneAxes that is the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrient.  When specifying Base, Plane is set on axis specified by PlaneAxes in the Base coordinate system.  When specifying Local, Plane is set on axis specified by PlaneAxes in the Local coordinate system of the number specified by PlaneRelativeRobotLocal.  When specifying Tool, Plane is set on axis specified by PlaneAxes in the Tool coordinate system.

PlaneAxes Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the axis to set Plane.  It is used when Relative is specified by PlaneType.  Plane is set on axis specified by PlaneAxes that is the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrient.  When specifying XY, Plane is set on XY plane in the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrient.  When specifying YZ, Plane is set on YZ plane in the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrient.  When specifying XZ, Plane is set on XZ plane in the coordinate system specified by PlaneRelativeOrient.

PlaneRelativeRobotLocal Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set Local number that specifies the relative position or direction of Plane.

It is used in either of the following cases;

Relative is specified in PlaneType and Local is specified in PlaneRelativeOrg

Relative is specified in PlaneType and Local is specified in PlaneRelativeOrient.
